title: 2028 Land Rover Range Rover GT
summary: What We Know So Far
whatHappening: Land Rover is developing a new 2028 Range Rover GT model that will expand its luxury SUV lineup. The vehicle is designed to compete in the high-performance luxury segment with distinctive styling and enhanced capabilities.
whoInvolved: Land Rover, the British luxury vehicle brand owned by Jaguar Land Rover (part of Tata Motors), is leading the development. The Range Rover nameplate has been Land Rover's flagship since 1970, representing the company's most prestigious and profitable vehicle line.
whyMatters: The GT variant signals Land Rover's commitment to capturing market share in the premium SUV segment where demand continues to grow. This new model could strengthen Land Rover's competitive position against rivals like BMW X7 and Mercedes-Benz GLS while boosting profitability through higher-priced variants.
moreContext: Land Rover has increasingly focused on creating multiple variants within the Range Rover family, including the Range Rover Sport and Range Rover Evoque, to reach different customer segments. The GT designation typically indicates a grand touring focus—combining performance with luxury and comfort for long-distance driving. This launch aligns with industry trends toward electrification and performance-focused variants of mainstream luxury models. The 2028 timeline suggests Land Rover is planning significant design and engineering work before the vehicle's market debut.

title: View Photos of the Land Rover Range Rover GT
summary: See interior and exterior photos of the new electric model from Land Rover, the Range Rover GT.
whatHappening: Land Rover has unveiled the Range Rover GT, a new electric vehicle that marks the automaker's push into the EV market. Photos of the vehicle's interior and exterior designs are now available for public viewing.
whoInvolved: Land Rover, the British luxury SUV brand owned by Jaguar Land Rover (part of India's Tata Motors), is introducing this new model. The company has a heritage spanning decades of producing premium off-road and luxury vehicles.
whyMatters: The Range Rover GT signals Land Rover's commitment to electrification as automakers face increasing pressure to shift away from internal combustion engines. This move could help the brand compete in the growing luxury EV segment while appealing to environmentally conscious buyers.
moreContext: Land Rover has long dominated the luxury SUV market with gas and diesel-powered Range Rovers. The automotive industry is experiencing rapid electrification, with traditional luxury brands like Mercedes, BMW, and Porsche launching premium EV models. Land Rover's parent company Tata Motors has been investing in electric vehicle technology globally. The GT nameplate indicates this model may target performance-oriented buyers seeking electric alternatives to traditional Range Rover offerings.

title: Range Rover Breaks the Mold and Goes Low with the New Electric GT
summary: The Range Rover GT prototype asks the question: what if a Range Rover were also a sedan?
whatHappening: Range Rover has unveiled a new electric GT prototype that reimagines the luxury SUV as a low-slung sedan. The concept vehicle blends Range Rover's DNA with sports car proportions, challenging traditional SUV design conventions.
whoInvolved: Range Rover, the iconic British luxury vehicle brand owned by Jaguar Land Rover, is pushing its electrification strategy with this experimental prototype. The move reflects the broader automotive industry's shift toward EV innovation and design reinvention.
whyMatters: This prototype signals that premium automakers are willing to radically reimagine heritage brands for the electric vehicle era. The concept could influence future luxury sedan and crossover designs, potentially capturing consumers seeking performance-oriented electric vehicles with Range Rover's prestige.
moreContext: Range Rover has dominated the luxury SUV market for decades but faces pressure from EV startups and competitors launching electric alternatives. Jaguar Land Rover has committed to an all-electric lineup by 2039, making concept vehicles like the GT essential for exploring new market segments. The sedan-like proportions represent a departure from the brand's traditional boxy silhouette, suggesting the company is testing whether luxury buyers will accept unconventional form factors if performance and technology deliver.

title: View Exterior Photos of the 2027 Kia Seltos
summary: See the exterior of the 2027 Kia Seltos from every angle.
whatHappening: Car and Driver has released exterior photos of the redesigned 2027 Kia Seltos, giving potential buyers their first comprehensive look at the compact SUV from multiple angles. The images showcase the vehicle's updated styling ahead of its official market debut.
whoInvolved: Kia Motors, the South Korean automaker, is behind the 2027 Seltos redesign. Car and Driver, a leading automotive media outlet, obtained and published the exclusive exterior photography.
whyMatters: The refreshed Seltos is crucial for Kia's competitive position in the crowded compact SUV segment, where models like the Honda HR-V and Toyota Corolla Cross dominate. Updated styling and design can significantly influence consumer purchasing decisions in this price-sensitive market category.
moreContext: The Seltos has been a key growth driver for Kia since its 2021 U.S. launch, appealing to younger buyers and first-time SUV customers with affordable pricing and modern features. The compact SUV segment has exploded over the past decade, now accounting for a substantial portion of vehicle sales globally. Regular design refreshes are essential in this category to maintain market relevance and keep pace with competitors introducing new generations. This 2027 model year update represents Kia's effort to maintain momentum in a segment where product cycles move quickly.

title: 2027 Mercedes-Maybach GLS-Class
summary: What We Know So Far
whatHappening: Mercedes-Benz is developing a Maybach version of its GLS-Class luxury SUV set to debut in 2027. This ultra-premium variant will sit at the top of the automaker's SUV lineup, combining the Maybach brand's exclusivity with the practical size of an SUV.
whoInvolved: Mercedes-Benz, the German luxury automaker, is behind this project through its Maybach sub-brand, which specializes in ultra-high-end vehicles positioned above the standard Mercedes offerings. Maybach has historically focused on sedans but is expanding into the SUV segment to capture growing demand.
whyMatters: This launch signals how luxury automakers are shifting toward SUVs as their flagship models, even for prestigious sub-brands typically associated with sedans. The Maybach GLS will compete directly with vehicles like the Rolls-Royce Cullinan and BMW's high-end X7, capturing wealthy buyers who prioritize size and presence over traditional sedan formats.
moreContext: Maybach was revived by Mercedes-Benz in 2021 after a 12-year hiatus, initially with the S-Class sedan as its only offering. The SUV market has dramatically reshaped luxury vehicle sales, with premium brands increasingly developing SUV models to meet consumer preferences for elevated driving positions and cargo space. Mercedes' current GLS-Class is already one of the brand's most popular models, and the Maybach variant will offer enhanced exclusivity, bespoke interiors, and performance tuning to justify premium pricing in the already-crowded ultra-luxury SUV segment.

title: View Exterior Photos of the 2027 Mercedes-Maybach GLS680
summary: See the outside of the new Mercedes-Maybach GLS6980 luxury SUV from every angle.
whatHappening: Mercedes-Benz has revealed exterior photos of the upcoming 2027 Mercedes-Maybach GLS680, its new ultra-luxury SUV. The vehicle showcases the brand's latest design language applied to the high-end sport utility segment.
whoInvolved: Mercedes-Benz, the German luxury automaker, is behind this release through its Maybach sub-brand, which focuses on ultra-premium vehicles for the world's wealthiest consumers. Car and Driver, a major automotive publication, is covering the debut imagery.
whyMatters: The GLS680 represents Mercedes' push deeper into the lucrative luxury SUV market, where competitors like Rolls-Royce (Cullinan) and Bentley (Bentayga) have found significant demand. This model could capture market share from ultra-wealthy buyers seeking flagship SUVs with uncompromising luxury credentials.
moreContext: The Maybach brand, revived in 2021 after a decade-long hiatus, has focused on limited-production sedans priced above $160,000. The GLS680 marks Maybach's entry into the SUV category, building on the success of Mercedes' standard GLS lineup while elevating it with exclusive Maybach styling, materials, and performance. The 2027 timeline suggests Mercedes is continuing its multi-year rollout of new Maybach models to re-establish the brand's position at the pinnacle of automotive luxury.

title: View Photos of the 1987 Nissan Sentra SE
summary: See the interior and exterior of the 1987 Nissan Sentra SE from every angle.
whatHappening: Car and Driver is showcasing the 1987 Nissan Sentra SE through a comprehensive photo gallery, capturing both interior and exterior details from multiple angles. The feature provides a visual tour of this classic compact car model.
whoInvolved: Car and Driver, a leading automotive enthusiast publication, is presenting the content. Nissan, the Japanese automaker, produced the Sentra SE as part of its popular compact car lineup.
whyMatters: The 1987 Sentra SE represents an important chapter in automotive history when Japanese manufacturers began dominating the compact car segment with affordable, reliable vehicles. This visual documentation helps automotive enthusiasts and historians appreciate the design details and engineering of a vehicle that helped establish Nissan's reputation for value-oriented performance.
moreContext: The 1987 Nissan Sentra was released during the second generation of the model (1982-1990), when the Sentra became a breakthrough success for Nissan in the U.S. market. The SE trim level represented the sportier, better-equipped variant of the lineup, featuring improved performance and interior appointments. By the late 1980s, Japanese compact cars like the Sentra were directly challenging American manufacturers by offering superior fuel efficiency, reliability, and affordability. The Sentra eventually became one of Nissan's best-selling models globally, contributing significantly to the brand's market expansion during this era.

title: Redesigned 2027 VW Atlas Arrives with a Notably Higher Base Price
summary: The Atlas enters its second generation with a revised four-cylinder engine and a more luxurious cabin, but that comes at a cost.
whatHappening: Volkswagen has unveiled the redesigned 2027 Atlas with a refreshed four-cylinder engine and upgraded interior appointments. The second-generation SUV arrives with a notably higher starting price than its predecessor.
whoInvolved: Volkswagen, the German automaker, is redesigning its three-row Atlas SUV, a key vehicle in its North American lineup. Car and Driver, a leading automotive publication, is reporting on the changes and pricing.
whyMatters: The price increase may pressure consumers in a competitive SUV market already dealing with inflation concerns. However, the upgraded cabin and revised powertrain could help VW justify the premium and attract buyers seeking more features in the midsize SUV segment.
moreContext: The Atlas is one of VW's most important vehicles for the North American market, competing against rivals like the Honda Pilot, Toyota Highlander, and Mazda CX-9. The second-generation redesign represents a significant update to the model since its 2018 introduction. Rising production costs and consumer demand for more luxurious interiors have prompted automakers across the industry to push pricing higher. The revised four-cylinder engine is part of VW's broader strategy to balance performance with efficiency amid changing emissions regulations.

title: View Photos of the 1993 Luxury Sedan Comparison Test
summary: See the interior and exterior of the 1993 Audi V8 Quattro, BMW 740i, Cadillac STS, Jaguar XJ6, Lexus LS400, and Mercedes-Benz 400E.
whatHappening: Car and Driver is showcasing a vintage 1993 luxury sedan comparison test through photos of six premium vehicles. The gallery displays both interior and exterior details of competing models from that era's most prestigious automakers.
whoInvolved: Car and Driver, a leading automotive publication, compiled this comparison featuring vehicles from Audi, BMW, Cadillac, Jaguar, Lexus, and Mercedes-Benz. These manufacturers represented the pinnacle of luxury automotive engineering in the early 1990s.
whyMatters: This retrospective comparison captures a pivotal moment when Japanese luxury brands like Lexus were beginning to challenge European dominance in the premium sedan segment. Understanding how these vehicles competed then provides insight into brand positioning and technology benchmarks that shaped the modern luxury market.
moreContext: The early 1990s marked a significant shift in luxury cars as Lexus's LS400 (launched in 1989) proved that Japanese manufacturers could build vehicles rivaling Mercedes-Benz on quality and reliability while undercutting prices. This era saw traditional European luxury makers like BMW, Mercedes, and Jaguar facing unprecedented competition, prompting innovation in comfort, technology, and engineering. The 1993 model year represented the height of analog luxury before rapid digitalization transformed the industry. Such comparisons were (and remain) essential automotive journalism, helping consumers navigate the substantial investment required to purchase vehicles in this premium category.

title: Tested: Dodge Charger R/T Is Heavier Than a Three-Row Mazda CX-90
summary: We aren't calling anyone overweight, but it's a little concerning that the eight-passenger SUV is lighter than the muscle car.
whatHappening: A new Dodge Charger R/T muscle car weighs more than a three-row Mazda CX-90 SUV, despite the SUV's larger passenger capacity. The comparison highlights how modern muscle cars have become unexpectedly heavy vehicles.
whoInvolved: Dodge, the American muscle car manufacturer owned by Stellantis, produces the Charger R/T. Mazda, the Japanese automaker, manufactures the CX-90 compact luxury SUV. Car and Driver, a leading automotive publication, conducted the weight comparison test.
whyMatters: The weight disparity raises concerns about fuel efficiency and performance in modern muscle cars, which have grown heavier with safety features and technology. This trend affects consumer choices between traditional muscle cars and practical family SUVs, as buyers increasingly prioritize efficiency and versatility. The finding underscores how automotive design priorities have shifted toward electrification and safety over lightweight construction.
moreContext: Modern muscle cars have added significant weight due to advanced safety systems, infotainment technology, emission controls, and structural reinforcement. The Charger R/T, a traditional V8-powered performance vehicle, represents the older generation of American muscle cars before the industry's shift toward electrification. Mazda's CX-90, meanwhile, incorporates lightweight engineering principles and is available with a plug-in hybrid option, reflecting contemporary efficiency priorities. This weight comparison exemplifies how the automotive industry has fundamentally transformed, with practical family vehicles now outperforming traditional performance cars in engineering efficiency.

title: From the Archive: 1993 Luxury Sedan Comparison Test
summary: Dining at the luxury sedan table? Bring lots of money; because it's all prime meat. We compare the Audi V8 Quattro, BMW 740i, Cadillac STS, Jaguar XJ6, Lexus LS400, and Mercedes-Benz 400E and choose the choicest cut.
whatHappening: Car and Driver is revisiting a 1993 luxury sedan comparison test that pitted six premium vehicles against each other to determine the best in class. The publication evaluated the Audi V8 Quattro, BMW 740i, Cadillac STS, Jaguar XJ6, Lexus LS400, and Mercedes-Benz 400E to crown the winner.
whoInvolved: Car and Driver, one of the automotive industry's most influential publications, conducted the test. The competing manufacturers represent different automotive philosophies: German engineering (Audi, BMW, Mercedes), Japanese precision (Lexus), British tradition (Jaguar), and American luxury (Cadillac).
whyMatters: This archival test captures a pivotal moment when Japanese luxury brands were challenging European dominance in the sedan market. The comparison reveals which vehicles best balanced performance, comfort, and value during the early 1990s economy—insights that shaped consumer preferences for years to come.
moreContext: The early 1990s marked a watershed moment for luxury sedans, with the Lexus LS400's 1989 debut fundamentally challenging established German and British marques by offering Japanese reliability at competitive prices. This era saw intense competition as manufacturers raced to define what modern luxury meant through advanced technology, performance, and refinement. The six vehicles tested represented dramatically different approaches: Audi and BMW emphasized driving dynamics and technology, Mercedes prioritized engineering excellence, Jaguar offered classic British elegance, Lexus focused on reliability and value, and Cadillac aimed to reclaim American luxury prestige. Such comprehensive comparison tests were crucial purchasing guides for affluent consumers navigating an increasingly crowded luxury market.

title: If This BMW M3 on BaT Was Good Enough for an IndyCar Racer, It's Good Enough for You
summary: The 2006 M3 that's up for auction has the Dinan S Competition package and was purchased new by two-time Indianapolis 500 winner Dan Wheldon.
whatHappening: A 2006 BMW M3 equipped with the Dinan S Competition package is currently up for auction on Bring a Trailer. The vehicle is noteworthy because it was originally purchased new by professional IndyCar racer Dan Wheldon.
whoInvolved: Dan Wheldon is a two-time Indianapolis 500 winner and accomplished IndyCar driver. BMW's M division and Dinan, a performance tuning specialist, are involved through the vehicle's high-performance modifications.
whyMatters: The car's provenance as a celebrity-owned performance vehicle likely increases its appeal and value to collectors. For buyers seeking a well-maintained, modified M3 with documented history from a professional driver, this auction represents an opportunity to own a piece of automotive credibility.
moreContext: The E92-generation BMW M3 (2006) is considered a modern classic among performance enthusiasts, featuring a naturally aspirated V8 engine. The Dinan S Competition package adds significant performance upgrades beyond factory specifications. Dan Wheldon's ownership connects the car to the world of professional motorsports, suggesting the vehicle may have been driven by someone with expertise in high-performance driving. Cars with verified celebrity or professional driver ownership typically command premium prices at auction compared to standard examples.

title: Bentley's EV Will Swap Faux Engine Noise for an Onboard Drum Track
summary: Crewe measured its most famous combustion engines and determined that the way to replace that soundtrack was old-fashioned percussion.
whatHappening: Bentley is replacing traditional engine sound simulation in its upcoming electric vehicle with an onboard drum track. The luxury automaker measured its famous combustion engines and determined that percussion instruments could authentically recreate that iconic acoustic signature.
whoInvolved: Bentley, the British luxury car manufacturer owned by Volkswagen Group, is developing this innovative audio solution for its EV lineup. The company is known for producing high-performance vehicles with distinctive engine characteristics that have defined its brand identity for over a century.
whyMatters: This approach addresses a growing challenge for luxury EV makers: how to preserve brand identity and driving experience when ditching internal combustion engines. For Bentley, maintaining sonic heritage is critical to justifying premium pricing and emotional connection to the brand, while the drum-track solution offers a more authentic alternative to artificial digital soundscaping that competitors use.
moreContext: As automakers transition to electric powertrains, they face pressure to retain the sensory experiences that define luxury and performance driving. Many manufacturers have experimented with synthesized engine sounds or speaker-based audio enhancement, but these often feel artificial to enthusiasts. Bentley's decision to base its EV soundtrack on actual measurements of its legendary engines—rather than digital simulation—reflects a commitment to authenticity that aligns with the brand's heritage-focused positioning. This strategy could influence how other luxury brands approach the challenge of preserving character in their electric futures.

title: Toyota Exec Says Japanese Automakers Need to Increase Cooperation
summary: Seeing the threat of looming Chinese competition on the horizon, Japan braces for the storm.
whatHappening: A Toyota executive is calling for increased cooperation among Japanese automakers to combat rising competition from Chinese manufacturers. Japan's auto industry is bracing for an intensifying competitive threat in the global market.
whoInvolved: Toyota, Japan's largest automaker, is signaling concern about Chinese competitors entering the market with aggressive pricing and EV offerings. The broader Japanese auto industry—including Honda, Nissan, and Mazda—faces similar pressures from Chinese rivals.
whyMatters: Increased cooperation could help Japanese automakers pool resources for EV development, battery technology, and supply chain resilience. Without coordination, individual Japanese companies risk losing market share to Chinese competitors who are moving faster and cheaper in key segments like electric vehicles.
moreContext: Chinese automakers like BYD have surpassed traditional Japanese and Western competitors in EV production and are aggressively expanding globally. Japan's auto industry has historically dominated global markets through superior quality and reliability, but Chinese manufacturers are closing the gap with lower costs and rapidly improving technology. The Japanese auto sector is simultaneously navigating the transition from internal combustion engines to electrification while facing import competition for the first time in decades. Industry consolidation and joint ventures have become common strategies for competing at scale in the EV era.

title: The Perfect First Car Is a Truck
summary: The Ford Maverick is versatile, safe, and—dare I say—cool. It's the perfect first car, and used ones get cheaper every day.
whatHappening: Car and Driver is making the case that the Ford Maverick represents an ideal entry-level vehicle for first-time car buyers. The publication highlights the truck's versatility, safety features, and appeal, while noting that used models are becoming increasingly affordable.
whoInvolved: Ford, the American automaker, produces the Maverick as part of its truck lineup. Car and Driver is a leading automotive publication that reviews and evaluates vehicles for consumers.
whyMatters: For budget-conscious first-time buyers, finding an affordable, practical vehicle is critical—and the Maverick offers utility without requiring a large investment. As used inventory grows and prices drop, the truck becomes an increasingly accessible option for younger drivers entering the market.
moreContext: The Ford Maverick, introduced in 2022, disrupted the truck market by offering an affordable compact pickup in an era of expensive, full-size models. The vehicle starts at a lower price point than many competitors and appeals to buyers seeking practicality without premium pricing. Used truck prices have been declining as more models enter the secondary market, improving affordability for first-time buyers. The Maverick combines truck functionality with fuel efficiency and modern safety technology, distinguishing it from traditional heavy-duty trucks that appeal mainly to professional users.

title: Head-Turning 1948 Mercury Woody 4x4 Wagon Is up for Sale on Bring a Trailer
summary: This four-wheel-drive wagon was a precursor to the modern SUV.
whatHappening: A rare 1948 Mercury Woody 4x4 wagon is being sold through Bring a Trailer, an online auction platform. This unique vehicle combines classic wood-paneled design with four-wheel-drive capability.
whoInvolved: Bring a Trailer is an online automotive auction platform specializing in collectible and classic vehicles. Mercury, Ford's mid-luxury brand, manufactured this wagon during the post-WWII era when such vehicles were experimental.
whyMatters: This sale highlights growing collector interest in early four-wheel-drive vehicles that presaged today's SUV market. The 1948 Mercury represents an overlooked chapter in automotive history when wagons with off-road capability were ahead of their time, making it historically significant for enthusiasts and market watchers.
moreContext: The 1948 Mercury Woody wagon is an exceptionally rare find—few automakers offered 4x4 wagons during this period, making this a precursor to modern sport utility vehicles. The woody aesthetic, featuring real or simulated wood paneling, was popular in the 1940s and represented premium, family-oriented transportation. Four-wheel-drive technology was primarily reserved for trucks and commercial vehicles at the time, making this wagon an unconventional combination. Bring a Trailer has become the premier marketplace for collector cars, regularly selling vehicles that command premium prices due to rarity and historical significance.

title: Dodge's Most Powerful Charger Sixpack Could Push 600 Horsepower
summary: While there's still no sign of a Hemi V-8, the Charger's six-cylinder variant is getting an upgrade that could substantially bump up the power.
whatHappening: Dodge is upgrading its Charger Sixpack six-cylinder engine to deliver around 600 horsepower, marking a significant power boost for the model. This enhancement comes as the automaker continues to develop its electric vehicle lineup while keeping performance-focused internal combustion options alive.
whoInvolved: Dodge, the American muscle car brand owned by Stellantis, is leading this engine development. The company has built its reputation on high-performance vehicles and is now pushing six-cylinder technology to compete with traditional V-8 expectations.
whyMatters: A 600-hp six-cylinder Charger could attract performance enthusiasts who want power without waiting for a traditional V-8 option that remains absent from Dodge's plans. This move signals Dodge's commitment to keeping combustion engines competitive as the industry shifts toward electrification, potentially extending the lifespan of its core performance franchise.
moreContext: Dodge has been repositioning itself as it transitions toward electrification, having already announced plans to phase out traditional engines. The Charger Sixpack was introduced as a six-cylinder alternative to fill the gap while customers awaited updates. The absence of a Hemi V-8 in the current generation has been a point of contention among purists, making this substantial six-cylinder upgrade a strategic move to maintain performance credibility during the brand's EV transition.

title: These Vehicles Are Dead for 2027
summary: Pour one out for greats like the BMW Z4, Lexus LC, Toyota GR Supra, and more.
whatHappening: Several beloved sports cars and specialty vehicles are being discontinued by major automakers, including the BMW Z4, Lexus LC, and Toyota GR Supra. These models won't be offered beyond 2027, marking the end of an era for performance-focused enthusiasts.
whoInvolved: BMW, Lexus (Toyota's luxury division), and Toyota are eliminating these nameplates from their lineups. These Japanese and German manufacturers have long positioned these vehicles as flagship performance and lifestyle products.
whyMatters: The discontinuations reflect automakers' shift toward electrification and profitability priorities, as traditional gas-powered sports cars face stricter emissions regulations and declining sales volumes. Enthusiasts are losing accessible, affordable performance options in an increasingly EV-dominated market.
moreContext: The automotive industry has aggressively pivoted toward electric vehicles to meet global emissions standards, making low-volume sports cars economically challenging to produce. The BMW Z4, Lexus LC, and Toyota GR Supra represented different market segments—roadster, luxury coupe, and lightweight sports car respectively—but all shared declining sales as consumers shifted toward SUVs and crossovers. Stricter upcoming emissions regulations in 2027 and beyond make it uneconomical to continue producing models with limited production runs. These discontinuations signal that traditional high-performance combustion vehicles are becoming luxury items reserved for ultra-premium brands, while mainstream manufacturers prioritize electrified alternatives.

title: Next-Generation BMW M3 Spotted Testing with Six-Cylinder Power
summary: BMW's iconic sports sedan will be offered with both electric and gas powertrains, and the six-cylinder M3 has now been spied testing at the Nürburgring.
whatHappening: BMW's next-generation M3 sports sedan is undergoing testing with a six-cylinder engine at the Nürburgring. The automaker is developing the iconic performance car with both traditional gas and electric powertrain options.
whoInvolved: BMW, the German luxury automaker known for its M performance division, is spearheading the M3's evolution. Car and Driver captured spy photos of the testing vehicle at Germany's famous racing circuit.
whyMatters: The M3's dual powertrain strategy reflects the automotive industry's shift toward electrification while maintaining performance credentials. This approach signals BMW's commitment to preserving its gas-engine heritage while meeting stricter emissions regulations and changing consumer preferences.
moreContext: The M3 has been a cornerstone of BMW's performance lineup since 1986, establishing the brand's reputation for high-performance sedans. Recent industry trends show luxury automakers increasingly offering hybrid and electric alternatives to traditionally gas-powered performance vehicles. BMW has already introduced electric versions in other segments, including the i7 and iX M60. The Nürburgring testing suggests BMW is validating both powertrains under demanding conditions to ensure comparable performance characteristics across the lineup.

title: Jensen Interceptor GTX Will Arrive as a Track-Only V-8 Bruiser
summary: Jensen International Automotive is reviving the iconic V-8 grand tourer, and the initially track-only Interceptor GTX will preview future road-going models.
whatHappening: Jensen International Automotive is reviving the legendary Interceptor nameplate with a new track-only V-8 model called the Interceptor GTX. The car will serve as a performance preview for future road-legal versions of the reborn grand tourer.
whoInvolved: Jensen International Automotive is the British automaker behind the revival of the iconic Interceptor brand, which was originally produced from 1966 to 1976. The company is working to resurrect one of the most celebrated V-8 grand tourers in automotive history.
whyMatters: The Interceptor GTX launch signals growing momentum in the retro-revival segment, where manufacturers are tapping nostalgia and performance enthusiasts' appetite for classic nameplates. Success with the track-focused model could validate demand for a road-going version, potentially establishing Jensen as a niche player competing against other bespoke automakers.
moreContext: The original Jensen Interceptor was a British grand tourer that gained cult status for its combination of style, performance, and luxury. Jensen ceased operations in 1976, leaving a 40+ year gap before the brand's revival. The track-only strategy mirrors industry trends seen with other heritage brands testing new models in motorsport before consumer launch. This approach allows Jensen to refine performance characteristics and generate buzz without the regulatory complexity of immediate road certification.

title: 2026 Lamborghini Temerario
summary: Review, Pricing, and Specs
whatHappening: Lamborghini is unveiling the 2026 Temerario, a new supercar model that marks a significant addition to the Italian automaker's lineup. The vehicle comes with detailed specifications, pricing information, and comprehensive reviews from automotive experts.
whoInvolved: Lamborghini, the Italian luxury supercar manufacturer owned by Audi (part of Volkswagen Group), is behind this release. Car and Driver, a leading automotive publication, is providing the official review and specifications.
whyMatters: The Temerario's debut signals Lamborghini's continued evolution in the ultra-premium supercar market as consumer preferences shift toward new performance standards and potential electrification. The pricing and specs will influence how competitors position their high-end offerings and set expectations for the next generation of exotic vehicles.
moreContext: Lamborghini has been refreshing its model lineup over the past few years, transitioning from purely internal combustion engines toward hybrid and electric powertrains. The automaker previously discontinued iconic models like the Aventador to make room for new designs that balance performance with evolving emissions standards. The Temerario name continues Lamborghini's tradition of naming cars after legendary bulls, maintaining brand heritage while signaling a modern direction. This release reflects the broader automotive industry trend of luxury makers launching new platforms to compete in an increasingly regulated and technology-driven market.

title: Honda Kills Off the Electric Prologue SUV After Just Three Years
summary: Based on a Chevy Blazer EV, the Prologue will not make it to the 2027 model year after sales dropped sharply in 2026.
whatHappening: Honda is discontinuing the electric Prologue SUV after just three model years, ending production before the 2027 model year arrives. The decision comes as sales for the vehicle plummeted in 2026.
whoInvolved: Honda, the Japanese automaker, developed the Prologue as part of its EV expansion strategy. The vehicle was based on General Motors' Chevrolet Blazer EV platform, representing a partnership between the two automotive giants.
whyMatters: The Prologue's early death signals challenges in Honda's electrification strategy and raises questions about the viability of platform-sharing agreements with GM. This reflects broader struggles in the EV market where consumer demand has softened and competition remains fierce, particularly in the crowded electric SUV segment.
moreContext: The Prologue launched for the 2024 model year as Honda's answer to the growing EV SUV market, leveraging GM's Ultium platform to accelerate development. Built on the same architecture as the Chevy Blazer EV and Equinox EV, the vehicle was positioned as a premium electric option. However, it faced stiff competition from established players and newer EV brands, and pricing pressures in the EV market intensified throughout 2025-2026. The sharp sales decline in 2026 apparently convinced Honda that continuing the model was no longer economically viable.

title: 'Conversational' AI Software Is Coming to Some Hondas and Acuras
summary: Nine Honda and three Acura models will get updated software featuring Google Gemini, an AI assistant that listens and talks to drivers in a conversational tone.
whatHappening: Honda and Acura are rolling out Google Gemini AI assistant software to 12 vehicle models combined. The conversational AI will let drivers interact with their cars through natural dialogue rather than traditional voice commands or touchscreen menus.
whoInvolved: Honda and Acura (luxury division of Honda) are partnering with Google to integrate Gemini, Google's latest AI assistant. Nine Honda models and three Acura models will receive the update, representing a significant deployment of advanced AI in mainstream vehicles.
whyMatters: This represents the automotive industry's shift toward conversational interfaces that could improve driver safety and convenience by reducing the need to take eyes off the road or hands off the wheel. It also signals how major automakers are betting on generative AI to differentiate their in-car experience as a competitive advantage in an increasingly software-driven market.
moreContext: Automakers have been gradually integrating voice assistants for years, but conversational AI powered by large language models like Gemini represents a generational leap in capability and natural interaction. Google has been aggressively positioning Gemini across consumer products as a differentiator from rivals like ChatGPT and Claude. This partnership follows the broader automotive trend of enhanced infotainment systems becoming key selling points, with companies like Tesla and legacy automakers investing heavily in software and AI capabilities to compete in the EV-dominated future.

title: This Crazy V-12-Powered Aston Martin SUV Is Ready for Battle
summary: The Dreadnought will exist solely in the virtual world of Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4, and it looks like an absolute beast.
whatHappening: Aston Martin has designed a V-12-powered SUV called the Dreadnought that's making its debut as a virtual vehicle in Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4. The beast-like SUV exists exclusively in the video game's virtual world.
whoInvolved: Aston Martin, the legendary British luxury automaker known for high-performance sports cars and James Bond vehicles, partnered with Activision's Call of Duty franchise. The collaboration brings the iconic car brand into the gaming space.
whyMatters: This partnership represents a growing trend of luxury brands leveraging gaming platforms to reach younger, digital-native audiences and build brand engagement beyond traditional automotive channels. For gamers, it adds exclusive branded content that enhances the Call of Duty experience with premium automotive design.
moreContext: Luxury and performance brands increasingly use video games as marketing channels, recognizing that gaming audiences represent a massive and valuable demographic. Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 continues the franchise's tradition of featuring premium branded vehicles and equipment. Aston Martin has a storied history of producing limited-edition and specially commissioned vehicles, making a virtual-exclusive design an unconventional but on-brand move. This type of in-game branded content has become standard practice in modern AAA gaming titles.

title: Infiniti Delays Sporty QX80 SUV to Inject Even More Performance
summary: The Red Sport was supposed to arrive this year, but now the hopped-up QX80 is being pushed back to 2028 as Infiniti extracts even more performance.
whatHappening: Infiniti is delaying its high-performance QX80 Red Sport SUV from its originally planned 2025 launch to 2028. The luxury automaker is using the extra time to enhance the vehicle's performance capabilities beyond its initial specifications.
whoInvolved: Infiniti, Nissan's luxury vehicle division, is behind the delay. The company has been working to establish itself as a competitor in the high-performance luxury SUV segment against brands like BMW and Mercedes-Benz.
whyMatters: The delay signals Infiniti's commitment to delivering a genuinely competitive performance vehicle rather than rushing a product to market. For consumers interested in sporty luxury SUVs, this pushes their availability timeline back three years but potentially delivers a more capable competitor to established rivals.
moreContext: The QX80 Red Sport was conceived as Infiniti's answer to other luxury performance SUVs in an increasingly crowded segment. The decision to delay reflects broader industry trends where manufacturers are investing heavily in performance tuning to differentiate their vehicles. Infiniti has faced challenges establishing itself as a performance-focused luxury brand and has been restructuring its lineup in recent years. This extended development timeline suggests the company believes additional engineering refinement is necessary to meet performance targets and justify the Red Sport's premium positioning.

title: 2026 Hyundai Ioniq 5 N's Price Cut Makes 641 HP Way More Affordable
summary: With the arrival of the Ioniq 6 N sedan still up in the air, Hyundai's OG performance EV is more enticing than it's ever been.
whatHappening: Hyundai has cut the price on the 2026 Ioniq 5 N, its high-performance electric vehicle that delivers 641 horsepower. The price reduction makes this performance EV significantly more competitive in the market.
whoInvolved: Hyundai, the South Korean automaker, is making the move with its performance-focused N division. The company is positioning the Ioniq 5 N as a flagship performance EV while the status of the upcoming Ioniq 6 N sedan remains uncertain.
whyMatters: The price cut removes a major barrier for consumers interested in high-powered electric vehicles, potentially driving sales and market share in the growing performance EV segment. This move could establish the Ioniq 5 N as the go-to performance EV option as competitors continue developing their own offerings.
moreContext: The Ioniq 5 N represents Hyundai's push into performance electrification, competing against vehicles like the BMW i4 M and Tesla Model Y Performance. With 641 horsepower and performance credentials built for track use, the vehicle targets enthusiasts willing to pay premium prices for EVs. The Ioniq 6 N sedan, which would expand Hyundai's N-badged EV lineup, has faced delays and uncertainty around its development and release timeline. This price cut suggests Hyundai is doubling down on the Ioniq 5 N as its primary performance EV offering while the sedan's future remains in question.

title: California DMV Demands Drivers Retake Test or Lose Their License
summary: The government agency says there's a possibility that flagged patterns show evidence of cheating.
whatHappening: California's Department of Motor Vehicles is ordering certain drivers to retake their licensing exams or face license revocation. The DMV says it detected suspicious patterns suggesting widespread cheating during test administration.
whoInvolved: The California DMV, the state agency responsible for issuing and managing driver's licenses, is initiating the crackdown. Affected drivers across the state are being notified of the requirement to retest.
whyMatters: This could result in thousands of drivers losing legal driving privileges if they don't comply with retesting. The move signals the DMV's effort to ensure road safety by validating that license holders actually meet competency standards, potentially preventing accidents caused by unqualified drivers.
moreContext: Driver's license testing has faced integrity challenges in various states, with reports of cheating rings and compromised testing facilities in recent years. California's DMV oversees millions of licenses and has faced criticism over testing center operations and oversight. The specific flagged patterns suggest either systematic cheating at particular testing locations or coordinated fraud schemes. This enforcement action represents an unusual step by a state DMV to retroactively validate licenses already issued.

title: Nissan Is Preparing to Scrap Altima Sedan as It Pares U.S. Lineup
summary: Nissan's sedan lineup is about to be a party of one, according to the brand's chief product officer in North America.
whatHappening: Nissan is discontinuing the Altima sedan, leaving only the Maxima in its U.S. sedan lineup. The automaker is streamlining its North American vehicle portfolio as consumer preferences shift away from traditional sedans.
whoInvolved: Nissan, a major Japanese automaker, is making this strategic decision through its North American leadership, specifically guidance from the brand's chief product officer. The move reflects broader industry consolidation among legacy automakers.
whyMatters: This signals Nissan's pivot toward SUVs and crossovers where market demand and profit margins are stronger. For sedan buyers, it reduces affordable midsize options in a shrinking segment, while for Nissan it's a calculated bet that trucks and SUVs represent its growth future.
moreContext: The Altima has been a cornerstone of Nissan's lineup for decades, but sedan sales have plummeted industrywide as consumers increasingly favor SUVs and crossovers. This mirrors decisions by competitors like Honda and Toyota, who have already culled sedan lineups. Nissan's move to prioritize SUVs and crossovers aligns with broader market trends where these vehicle segments now dominate U.S. sales, with sedans representing less than 25% of the market compared to over 50% two decades ago.

title: Goodwood Festival of Speed Blends Competition, Exhibition, and Exhilaration
summary: What started as a one-day race up a driveway is now one of the biggest events on the calendar for hypercar reveals and motorsport celebrity appearances.
whatHappening: The Goodwood Festival of Speed is one of the automotive calendar's premier events, blending high-speed competition with exclusive hypercar debuts and star-studded motorsport appearances. The multi-day spectacle transforms a British estate into a showcase for cutting-edge performance vehicles and racing talent.
whoInvolved: Goodwood, a historic English estate, hosts the festival as a platform for luxury automakers, hypercar manufacturers, and professional racing drivers to display their latest innovations and achievements.
whyMatters: The festival has become the definitive venue for hypercar manufacturers to reveal next-generation vehicles to global media and enthusiasts, influencing industry narratives and consumer interest. Its growth signals the automotive industry's continued investment in high-performance experiences and exclusive events as marketing channels.
moreContext: The festival originated as a modest one-day race held on the Goodwood estate's private driveway, establishing its unique venue and intimate-yet-grand character. Over decades, it evolved into a multi-day international event attracting hundreds of thousands of attendees and becoming a mandatory calendar fixture for luxury brands launching flagship models. The event reflects broader automotive trends toward experiential marketing and the sustained cultural cachet of motorsport in brand positioning. Goodwood's combination of competitive racing, static exhibitions, and exclusive debuts has created a model that competitors struggle to replicate, cementing its status as the premier automotive gathering outside Formula 1 and traditional motorsport championships.

title: View Photos of the 2026 Goodwood Festival of Speed
summary: See photos of the 2026 Goodwood Festival of Speed from every angle.
whatHappening: Car and Driver is releasing a photo gallery showcasing the 2026 Goodwood Festival of Speed. The collection captures vehicles and action from multiple angles at the prestigious automotive event.
whoInvolved: Car and Driver, a leading automotive publication, is documenting the 2026 Goodwood Festival of Speed—an annual showcase held at Goodwood House in West Sussex, England that attracts manufacturers, collectors, and racing teams from around the world.
whyMatters: The photo coverage provides enthusiasts with visual access to rare vehicles, concept cars, and performance machines that appear at one of the automotive world's most exclusive events. This documentation helps automotive fans and industry observers stay informed about new reveals and classic car presentations without attending in person.
moreContext: The Goodwood Festival of Speed, held annually since 1993, is one of the world's largest automotive events, known for its hillclimb races and exclusive vehicle debuts. The event attracts manufacturers showcasing everything from hypercars to restored classics, and it serves as a major platform for automotive announcements. The 2026 edition continues this tradition of bringing together the automotive industry's most significant players and most coveted vehicles in one location.
